anomalies.what_is_it: A credential attack event was detected from 10.0.0.9 targeting 172.16.0.30:3389 over TCP.
anomalies.current_impact: Active authentication pressure on exposed services. Critical admin/remote services are being targeted now. Network performance degradation and service instability may occur. Access control is already rejecting suspicious sessions.
anomalies.future_impact: Account takeover, privilege escalation, and ransomware staging. Without action, attackers may gain valid credentials within hours.
risk_reasons: Sensitive service with unusually high transfer
recommended_fix_steps: Validate whether this traffic is expected by checking asset owner and change logs.
If unexpected, isolate the source host from critical network segments immediately.
Block or rate-limit suspicious source IP and destination port on firewall/IPS.
Force password reset and enable MFA if repeated login failures are present.
Run endpoint malware scan and review active processes on the source host.
Add a detection rule for this behavior pattern and monitor for recurrence for 72 hours.
